citeinfo.title: Supplementary Data Spanning 1951-2100 to Support the PRMS CONUS Futures Portal

pubinfo.pubplace: USGS National Climate Adaptation Science Center

descript.abstract: This data release contains data used within the PRMS CONUS Futures Portal including descriptions of data layers in the Uncertainty Analysis section, reference model performance at stream gage locations, and model performance metrics for climate model based simulations. 1) The description of variables available in the Uncertainty Analysis tab include variable short name, variable long name, description, methodology, caveats, additional user information, and references; 2) stream gage validation metrics calculated at 8720 USGS stream gages for 1951-2009 with observed flow compared to reference model flow (Maurer). Information provided at each gage location includes corresponding PRMS segment ID, reference or non-reference gage status, drainage basin area comparison, Nash-Sutcliffe Efficiency (NSE), Percent Bias (Pbias), Root Mean Standard Deviation Ratio (RSR), a cumulative gage score summing NSE, Pbias, and RSR suitability, and Disturbance Index (DI); and 3) ensemble model performance metrics calculated using the Kolmogorov-Smirnov statistical test. Values are provided for the reference model simulation versus the historical global climate model simulations (1976-2005) and for the historical versus future global climate model simulations at three future time periods (2015-2045, 2046-2075, and 2071-2100) versus the historical period (1976-2005).
descript.purpose: This data is being made available to support the PRMS CONUS Futures Portal to provide access to data that is either 1) not currently available or 2) not easily reproduced by other publicly available code without significant computing resources.
descript.supplinf: For more information about preparation of these data, please see the open-source software project here: https://code.usgs.gov/casc/cats/ecosystems-prms-futures-portal-supplementary. Please note that this software is preliminary or provisional and is subject to revision. It is being provided to meet the need for timely best science. The software has not received final approval by the U.S. Geological Survey (USGS). No warranty, expressed or implied, is made by the USGS or the U.S. Government as to the functionality of the software and related material nor shall the fact of release constitute any such warranty. The software is provided on the condition that neither the USGS nor the U.S. Government shall be held liable for any damages resulting from the authorized or unauthorized use of the software.

idinfo.accconst: No access constraints. Please see 'Distribution Information' for details.
idinfo.useconst: These data are marked with a Creative Common CC0 1.0 Universal License. These data are in the public domain and do not have any use constraints. Users are advised to read the dataset's metadata thoroughly to understand appropriate use and data limitations.

cntaddr.address: 3916 Sunset Ridge Rd, Geological Surv Bldg

idinfo.datacred: The development of this data was supported by Jacob H. LaFontaine.
idinfo.native: The dataset was created using MATLAB (R2024b; The MathWorks Inc.), high-performance computing resources (Falgout et al. n.d.), and R Statistical Software (v4.5.0; R Core Team 2025).

citeinfo.title: R: A Language and Environment for Statistical Computing
Simulation of water availability in the Southeastern United States for historical and potential future climate and land-cover conditions

serinfo.sername: U.S. Geological Survey Scientific Investigations Report

citeinfo.title: Model Evaluation Guidelines for Systematic Quantification of Accuracy in Watershed Simulations

pubinfo.publish: Transactions of the American Society of Agricultural and Biological Engineers

citeinfo.title: GAGES-II: Geospatial Attributes of Gages for Evaluating Streamflow
MATLAB Hydrological Index Tool (MHIT): A high performance library to calculate 171 ecologically relvant hydrological indices
hydroGOF: Goodness-of-fit functions for comparison of simulated and observed hydrological time series

attracc.attraccr: The files were subject to USGS peer review but no formal attribute accuracy tests were conducted. Underlying datasets cited here also underwent USGS peer review and approval processes. Data was plotted and inspected for accuracy. This data continues to undergo analytical checks and validation, and the code used for production is available for review and reproduction.

dataqual.logic: No formal logical accuracy tests were conducted. The values have been checked for missing values and abnormalities/anomalies. Data ranges were also checked for consistency and realism. Data are as expected with no identified duplicates or outliers. Missing values and ranges are described in the metadata record.
dataqual.complete: Data set is considered complete for the information presented, as described in the abstract. Users are advised to read the rest of the metadata record carefully for additional details. Data are available for 8270 stream gage locations and other analysis is available for 56,460 stream segments.

horizpa.horizpar: A formal accuracy assessment of the horizontal positional information in the data set has not been conducted.

vertacc.vertaccr: A formal accuracy assessment of the vertical positional information in the data set has either not been conducted, or is not applicable.

citeinfo.title: Application of the National Hydrologic Model Infrastructure with the Precipitation-Runoff Modeling System (NHM-PRMS),1980-2016, Daymet Version 3 calibration
Application of the National Hydrologic Model Infrastructure with the Precipitation-Runoff Modeling System (NHM-PRMS), 1950-2010, Maurer Calibration

srcinfo.srccontr: Uses parameter files from child item Input Data for Hydrologic Simulations of the CONUS using the NHM-PRMS, 1950-2010, Maurer Calibration as information for stream gage correspondence with PRMS stream segments.

citeinfo.title: Model Input and Output for Hydrologic Simulations for the Conterminous United States for Historical and Future Conditions Using the National Hydrologic Model Infrastructure (NHM) and the Coupled Model Intercomparison Project Phase 5 (CMIP5), 1950-2100

srcinfo.srccontr: Daily streamflow data is used to calculate gage performance and annual streamflow statistics for Kolmogorov-Smirnov testing. Streamflow variables are defined in "streamflow_statistics_description_table.csv"

procstep.procdesc:
Development of Stat_gage_performance_conus_new_1951_2009 and Dyn_gage_performance_conus_new_1951_2009 files:
Gage performance uses daily reference (Maurer) streamflow data from LaFontaine and Riley, 2023a, stream gage information from LaFontaine et al. 2023b, and stream gage information from Hay et al. 2020.
Calculations also require daily streamflow and information from USGS gages, downloaded using the dataRetrieval R package (De Cicco, L.A., Hirsch, R.M., Lorenz, D., Watkins, W.D., Johnson, M., 2025, dataRetrieval: R packages for discovering and retrieving water data available from Federal hydrologic web services, v.2.7.20, doi:10.5066/P9X4L3GE).
Daily Streamflow from stream gages and daily (Maurer) reference streamflow data is used to calculate Nash-Sutcliffe Efficiency, Percent Bias, and Root Mean Standard Deviation Ratio (RSR) using the HydroGOF package in R (Zambrano-Bigiarini, 2024). Values are assessed using thresholds in Lafontaine et al. 2019 and Moriasi et al. 2007 and assigned a 1 (Satisfactory) or 0 (Un-Satisfactory). An overall gage score is calculated by summing the three individual scores.
More information and code can be found with the PRMS CONUS Futures Portal Supplementary Code Open-Source Software Project at https://code.usgs.gov/casc/cats/ecosystems-prms-futures-portal-supplementary.

Development of files in child items Historical_KS and Future KS:
Annual values of each PRMS Streamflow Statistics using daily output data in MATLAB (LaFontaine and Riley, 2023a).
Two sample KS testing was performed using ks.test (stats package) in R. Segments with missing data during the period of analysis are omitted and receive an NA value. Date ranges for calculation are included in the title of the files. KS testing was performed on each time period, emissions scenario, stream segment, PRMS Streamflow Statistic, simulation type (static and dynamic) and global climate model combination. Final files are summed across all 13 global climate models for each time period, emissions scenario, stream segment, PRMS Streamflow Statistic, and simulation type (static and dynamic).
More information and code can be found with the PRMS CONUS Futures Portal Supplementary Code Open-Source Software Project at https://code.usgs.gov/casc/cats/ecosystems-prms-futures-portal-supplementary.

enttyp.enttypd: Comma Separated Value (CSV) file containing information about data layers included in the PRMS CONUS Futures Portal.

attr.attrdef: Variable short name used in code

attrdomv.udom: text describing the variable short name used in the code

attr.attrdef: Variable long name

attrdomv.udom: text describing the variable long name

attr.attrdef: Description of the variable including ranges, time periods, references, and definitions.

attrdomv.udom: text describing the variable including ranges, time periods, references, and definitions.

attr.attrdef: Description of the calculation methodology for each variable if applicable. Source data and method citations are also included.

attrdomv.udom: text describing calculation methodology for each variable if applicable. Source data and method citations are also included.

attr.attrdef: Description of potential caveats for variable calculation, assumptions in methodology, and issues that potential users should be aware of.

attrdomv.udom: text describing potential caveats for variable calculation, assumptions in methodology, and issues that potential users should be aware of.

attr.attrdef: Any additional information helpful to users that is not included in other columns.

attrdomv.udom: text describing any additional information helpful to users that is not included in other columns.

attr.attrdef: Full reference information for in-text citations in other columns.

attrdomv.udom: text with full reference information for in-text citations in other columns.

enttyp.enttypd: Comma Separated Value (CSV) file containing data comparing observed flow data at USGS streamgages to reference (Maurer) simulation data. This record description also applies to Stat_gage_performance_conus_new_1951_2009.csv. The first term in the filename refers to the simulation type (Dyn = Dynamic, Stat = Static). The dates (1951-2009) refer to the period of calculation.

attr.attrdef: USGS stream gage ID
PRMS segment ID
Reference or Non-Reference status of gage based on the GAGES-II dataset (Falcone, 2011). This data is ingested from Hay et al. 2020.

edom.edomvd: No value in the underlying source data, originally marked as -999.

attr.attrdef: Percent difference in drainage basin area of areas associated with gages between the USGS National Water Information System (NWISda) and the USGS NHM Geospatial Fabric (GFda). Original drainage area data from Hay et al. 2020. Values can range from negative infinity to infinity.

edom.edomvd: No Data. Values are either missing in the underlying data or input drainage areas are 0 or -999.

attr.attrdef: Nash-Sutcliffe Efficiency. Values can range from negative infinity to 1.

edom.edomvd: No Data. Value was not able to be calculated from the flow record due to missing data.

attr.attrdef: Percent Bias. Values can range from -100 to infinity.

edom.edomvd: No Data. Value was not able to be calculated from the flow record due to missing data.

attr.attrdef: Root Mean Standard Deviation Ratio (RSR). Values can range from 0 to infinity.

edom.edomvd: No Data. Value was not able to be calculated from the flow record due to missing data.

attr.attrdef: Absolute value of percent bias. Values can range from 0 to infinity.

edom.edomvd: No Data. Value was not able to be calculated from the flow record due to missing data.

attr.attrdef: Evaluation score of Nash-Sutcliffe Efficiency calculation. Thresholds based on LaFontaine et al. 2019 and Moriasi et al. 2007.

edom.edomvd: Satisfactory performance. Gage value greater than or equal to 0.50
Un-Satisfactory performance. Gage value less than 0.50 or NA

attr.attrdef: Evaluation score of Absolute bias calculation. Thresholds based on LaFontaine et al. 2019 and Moriasi et al. 2007.

edom.edomvd: Satisfactory performance. Gage value less than 25.
Un-Satisfactory performance. Gage value greater than or equal to 25 or NA

attr.attrdef: Evaluation score of RSR calculation. Thresholds based on LaFontaine et al. 2019 and Moriasi et al. 2007.

edom.edomvd: Satisfactory performance. Gage value less than 0.7000000.
Un-Satisfactory performance. Gage value greater than or equal to 0.7000000 or NA

attr.attrdef: Sum of individual score columns NWEeval, AbsPbiaseval, and RSReval.
Disturbance Index as defined in GAGES-II (Falcone, 2011). Original drainage area data from Hay et al. 2020. Values can range from 1 to 42.

edom.edomvd: No value in the underlying source data, originally marked as -999.

enttyp.enttypd:
Comma Separated Value (CSV) file. Values contain the number of models in the ensemble (n=13) where a comparison between the reference (Maurer) simulation data and the historical global climate model (GCM) data from 1976-2005 failed to reject the null hypothesis in a two-sample Kolmogorov-Smirnov test. These record descriptions apply to all files with "KSHistSig" including 1) dynamic_KSHistSig_historical_r1i1p1_1976_2005_1976_2005_diff.csv; 2) static_KSHistSig_historical_r1i1p1_1976_2005_1976_2005_diff.csv.
The terms in the filename separated by underscores (_) detail: 1. The simulation type, static or dynamic 2. the period of analysis, historical 3. the realization, r1i1p1 4. the time period of analysis, 1976-2005

attr.attrdef: PRMS segment ID
Models meeting criteria in dataset description for PRMS streamflow statistic ma3 (Coefficient of variation (standard deviation/mean) for each year).

Models meeting criteria in dataset description for PRMS streamflow statistic ma4 (Standard deviation of the percentiles of the logs of the entire flow record divided by the mean of percentiles of the logs).
Models meeting criteria in dataset description for PRMS streamflow statistic ma12 (Mean of monthly flow values for January).
Models meeting criteria in dataset description for PRMS streamflow statistic ma13 (Mean of monthly flow values for February).
Models meeting criteria in dataset description for PRMS streamflow statistic ma14 (Mean of monthly flow values for March).
Models meeting criteria in dataset description for PRMS streamflow statistic ma15 (Mean of monthly flow values for April).
Models meeting criteria in dataset description for PRMS streamflow statistic ma16 (Mean of monthly flow values for May).
Models meeting criteria in dataset description for PRMS streamflow statistic ma17 (Mean of monthly flow values for June).
Models meeting criteria in dataset description for PRMS streamflow statistic ma18 (Mean of monthly flow values for July).
Models meeting criteria in dataset description for PRMS streamflow statistic ma19 (Mean of monthly flow values for August).
Models meeting criteria in dataset description for PRMS streamflow statistic ma20 (Mean of monthly flow values for September).
Models meeting criteria in dataset description for PRMS streamflow statistic ma21 (Mean of monthly flow values for October).
Models meeting criteria in dataset description for PRMS streamflow statistic ma22 (Mean of monthly flow values for November).
Models meeting criteria in dataset description for PRMS streamflow statistic ma23 (Mean of monthly flow values for December).
Models meeting criteria in dataset description for PRMS streamflow statistic mh14 (Median of annual maximum flows).
Models meeting criteria in dataset description for PRMS streamflow statistic mh20 (Specific mean annual maximum flow).
Models meeting criteria in dataset description for PRMS streamflow statistic ml17 (Base flow).
Models meeting criteria in dataset description for PRMS streamflow statistic dh1 (Annual maximum daily flow).
Models meeting criteria in dataset description for PRMS streamflow statistic dh2 (Annual maximum of 3-day moving average flows).
Models meeting criteria in dataset description for PRMS streamflow statistic dh3 (Annual maximum of 7-day moving average flows).
Models meeting criteria in dataset description for PRMS streamflow statistic dh4 (Annual maximum of 30-day moving average flows).
Models meeting criteria in dataset description for PRMS streamflow statistic dh5 (Annual maximum of 90-day moving average flows).
Models meeting criteria in dataset description for PRMS streamflow statistic dh15 (High flow pulse duration)
Models meeting criteria in dataset description for PRMS streamflow statistic dl1 (Annual minimum daily flow).
Models meeting criteria in dataset description for PRMS streamflow statistic dl2 (Annual minimum of 3-day moving average flow).
Models meeting criteria in dataset description for PRMS streamflow statistic dl3 (Annual minimum of 7-day moving average flow).
Models meeting criteria in dataset description for PRMS streamflow statistic dl4 (Annual minimum of 30-day moving average flow).
Models meeting criteria in dataset description for PRMS streamflow statistic dl5 (Annual minimum of 90-day moving average flow)
Models meeting criteria in dataset description for PRMS streamflow statistic dl16 (Low flow pulse duration)
Models meeting criteria in dataset description for PRMS streamflow statistic fh1 (High flood pulse count).

Models meeting criteria in dataset description for PRMS streamflow statistic fh5 (Flood frequency. Compute the average number of flow events with flows above a threshold equal to the median flow value for the entire flow record).

Models meeting criteria in dataset description for PRMS streamflow statistic fh6 (Flood frequency. Compute the average number of flow events with flows above a threshold equal to three times the median flow value for the entire flow record.).

Models meeting criteria in dataset description for PRMS streamflow statistic fh7 (Flood frequency. Compute the average number of flow events with flows above a threshold equal to seven times the median flow value for the entire flow record.).

Models meeting criteria in dataset description for PRMS streamflow statistic fl1 (Low flood pulse count. Compute the average number of flow events with flows below a threshold equal to the 25th percentile value for the entire flow record.).

Models meeting criteria in dataset description for PRMS streamflow statistic fl3 (Frequency of low pulse spells. Compute the average number of flow events with flows below a threshold equal to 5 percent of the mean flow value for the entire flow record).
Models meeting criteria in dataset description for PRMS streamflow statistic th1 (Julian date of annual maximum).
Models meeting criteria in dataset description for PRMS streamflow statistic tl1 (Julian date of annual minimum)
Models meeting criteria in dataset description for PRMS streamflow statistic ra1 (Rise rate. The change in flow for days in which the change is positive for the entire flow record.)
Models meeting criteria in dataset description for PRMS streamflow statistic ra3 (Fall rate. The change in flow for days in which the change is negative for the entire flow record).
Models meeting criteria in dataset description for PRMS streamflow statistic ra8 (Number of reversals).
Models meeting criteria in dataset description for PRMS streamflow statistic spr_mag (Specific mean spring (April-June) maximum flow).
Models meeting criteria in dataset description for PRMS streamflow statistic spr_dur3v2 (Spring (April-June) maximum of 3-day moving average flows).
Models meeting criteria in dataset description for PRMS streamflow statistic spr_dur7v2 (Spring (April-June) maximum of 7-day moving average flows).
Models meeting criteria in dataset description for PRMS streamflow statistic spr_freq (Flood frequency for April-June)
Models meeting criteria in dataset description for PRMS streamflow statistic spr_ord (Julian date of spring (April-June) maximum).

Models meeting criteria in dataset description for PRMS streamflow statistic sum_mag (Minimum of the summer (July-September) flows divided by the drainage area (cubic feet per second/square mile - temporal)).
Models meeting criteria in dataset description for PRMS streamflow statistic sum_dur3v2 (Summer (July-September) minimum of 3-day moving average flow).
Models meeting criteria in dataset description for PRMS streamflow statistic sum_dur7v2 (Summer (July-September) minimum of 7-day moving average flow).
Models meeting criteria in dataset description for PRMS streamflow statistic sum_freq (Flood frequency for July-September).
Models meeting criteria in dataset description for PRMS streamflow statistic sum_ord (Julian date of summer (July-September) minimum).
Models meeting criteria in dataset description for PRMS streamflow statistic sum_cv (Coefficient of variation (standard deviation/mean) for each year for the summer (July-September)).
Models meeting criteria in dataset description for PRMS streamflow statistic lf1 (Number of days per year below a threshold of 0.1 cubic feet per second per square mile)

enttyp.enttypd:
Comma Separated Value (CSV) file. Values contain the number of models in the ensemble (n=13) where a comparison between the historical global climate model (GCM) data and the future GCM data rejected the null hypothesis in a two-sample Kolmogorov-Smirnov test. These record descriptions apply to all files with "KSFutSig" including 1)dynamic_KSFutureSig_rcp45_r1i1p1_2016_2045_1976_2005_diff.csv; 2) dynamic_KSFutureSig_rcp45_r1i1p1_2046_2075_1976_2005_diff.csv; 3) dynamic_KSFutureSig_rcp45_r1i1p1_2071_2100_1976_2005_diff.csv; 4) dynamic_KSFutureSig_rcp85_r1i1p1_2016_2045_1976_2005_diff.csv; 5) dynamic_KSFutureSig_rcp85_r1i1p1_2046_2075_1976_2005_diff.csv; 6) dynamic_KSFutureSig_rcp85_r1i1p1_2071_2100_1976_2005_diff.csv; 7) static_KSFutureSig_rcp45_r1i1p1_2016_2045_1976_2005_diff.csv 8) static_KSFutureSig_rcp45_r1i1p1_2046_2075_1976_2005_diff.csv; 9) static_KSFutureSig_rcp45_r1i1p1_2071_2100_1976_2005_diff.csv; 10) static_KSFutureSig_rcp85_r1i1p1_2016_2045_1976_2005_diff.csv; 11) static_KSFutureSig_rcp85_r1i1p1_2046_2075_1976_2005_diff.csv; 12) static_KSFutureSig_rcp85_r1i1p1_2071_2100_1976_2005_diff.csv
The terms in the filename separated by underscores (_) detail: 1. The simulation type, static or dynamic 2. The information provided, KSFutureSig 3. The emissions scenario, rcp45 or rcp85 4. the realization, r1i1p1 5. the future time period of analysis, 2016-2045, 2046-2075, or 2071-2100 6. The historical reference period, 1976-2005 7. Indication that results show the significance of the difference between the two periods, diff.

attr.attrdef: PRMS segment ID
Models meeting criteria in dataset description for PRMS streamflow statistic ma3 (Coefficient of variation (standard deviation/mean) for each year).

Models meeting criteria in dataset description for PRMS streamflow statistic ma4 (Standard deviation of the percentiles of the logs of the entire flow record divided by the mean of percentiles of the logs).
Models meeting criteria in dataset description for PRMS streamflow statistic ma12 (Mean of monthly flow values for January).
Models meeting criteria in dataset description for PRMS streamflow statistic ma13 (Mean of monthly flow values for February).
Models meeting criteria in dataset description for PRMS streamflow statistic ma14 (Mean of monthly flow values for March).

Models meeting criteria in dataset description for PRMS streamflow statistic ma17 (Mean of monthly flow values for June).

Models meeting criteria in dataset description for PRMS streamflow statistic ma19 (Mean of monthly flow values for August).
Models meeting criteria in dataset description for PRMS streamflow statistic ma20 (Mean of monthly flow values for September).
Models meeting criteria in dataset description for PRMS streamflow statistic ma21 (Mean of monthly flow values for October).
Models meeting criteria in dataset description for PRMS streamflow statistic ma22 (Mean of monthly flow values for November).
Models meeting criteria in dataset description for PRMS streamflow statistic ma23 (Mean of monthly flow values for December).
Models meeting criteria in dataset description for PRMS streamflow statistic mh14 (Median of annual maximum flows).
Models meeting criteria in dataset description for PRMS streamflow statistic mh20 (Specific mean annual maximum flow).
Models meeting criteria in dataset description for PRMS streamflow statistic ml17 (Base flow).
Models meeting criteria in dataset description for PRMS streamflow statistic dh1 (Annual maximum daily flow).
Models meeting criteria in dataset description for PRMS streamflow statistic dh2 (Annual maximum of 3-day moving average flows).
Models meeting criteria in dataset description for PRMS streamflow statistic dh3 (Annual maximum of 7-day moving average flows).

Models meeting criteria in dataset description for PRMS streamflow statistic dl1 (Annual minimum daily flow).
Models meeting criteria in dataset description for PRMS streamflow statistic dl2 (Annual minimum of 3-day moving average flow).
Models meeting criteria in dataset description for PRMS streamflow statistic dl3 (Annual minimum of 7-day moving average flow).
Models meeting criteria in dataset description for PRMS streamflow statistic dl4 (Annual minimum of 30-day moving average flow)
Models meeting criteria in dataset description for PRMS streamflow statistic dl5 (Annual minimum of 90-day moving average flow)
Models meeting criteria in dataset description for PRMS streamflow statistic dl16 (Low flow pulse duration)
Models meeting criteria in dataset description for PRMS streamflow statistic fh1 (High flood pulse count).

Models meeting criteria in dataset description for PRMS streamflow statistic fh5 (Flood frequency. Compute the average number of flow events with flows above a threshold equal to the median flow value for the entire flow record)

Models meeting criteria in dataset description for PRMS streamflow statistic fh6 (Flood frequency. Compute the average number of flow events with flows above a threshold equal to three times the median flow value for the entire flow record.).

Models meeting criteria in dataset description for PRMS streamflow statistic fh7 (Flood frequency. Compute the average number of flow events with flows above a threshold equal to seven times the median flow value for the entire flow record.).

Models meeting criteria in dataset description for PRMS streamflow statistic fl1 (Low flood pulse count. Compute the average number of flow events with flows below a threshold equal to the 25th percentile value for the entire flow record.).

Models meeting criteria in dataset description for PRMS streamflow statistic fl3 (Frequency of low pulse spells. Compute the average number of flow events with flows below a threshold equal to 5 percent of the mean flow value for the entire flow record).
Models meeting criteria in dataset description for PRMS streamflow statistic th1 (Julian date of annual maximum).
Models meeting criteria in dataset description for PRMS streamflow statistic tl1 (Julian date of annual minimum)
Models meeting criteria in dataset description for PRMS streamflow statistic ra1 (Rise rate. The change in flow for days in which the change is positive for the entire flow record.)
Models meeting criteria in dataset description for PRMS streamflow statistic ra3 (Fall rate. The change in flow for days in which the change is negative for the entire flow record).
Models meeting criteria in dataset description for PRMS streamflow statistic ra8 (Number of reversals).
Models meeting criteria in dataset description for PRMS streamflow statistic spr_mag (Specific mean spring (April-June) maximum flow).
Models meeting criteria in dataset description for PRMS streamflow statistic spr_dur3v2 (Spring (April-June) maximum of 3-day moving average flows)
Models meeting criteria in dataset description for PRMS streamflow statistic spr_dur7v2 (Spring (April-June) maximum of 7-day moving average flows).
Models meeting criteria in dataset description for PRMS streamflow statistic spr_freq (Flood frequency for April-June).
Models meeting criteria in dataset description for PRMS streamflow statistic spr_ord (Julian date of spring (April-June) maximum).

Models meeting criteria in dataset description for PRMS streamflow statistic sum_mag (Minimum of the summer (July-September) flows divided by the drainage area (cubic feet per second/square mile - temporal)).
Models meeting criteria in dataset description for PRMS streamflow statistic sum_dur3v2 (Summer (July-September) minimum of 3-day moving average flow).
Models meeting criteria in dataset description for PRMS streamflow statistic sum_dur7v2 (Summer (July-September) minimum of 7-day moving average flow).
Models meeting criteria in dataset description for PRMS streamflow statistic sum_freq (Flood frequency for July-September).
Models meeting criteria in dataset description for PRMS streamflow statistic sum_ord (Julian date of summer (July-September) minimum).
Models meeting criteria in dataset description for PRMS streamflow statistic sum_cv (Coefficient of variation (standard deviation/mean) for each year for the summer (July-September)).
Models meeting criteria in dataset description for PRMS streamflow statistic lf1 (Number of days per year below a threshold of 0.1 cubic feet per second per square mile).

overview.eaover: The zipped folder Historical_KS contains two files with the historical model versus reference Kolmogorov-Smirnov ensemble totals for the two simulation types: dynamic and static for the period 1976-2005. The zipped folder Future_KS contains twelve files with the historical (1976-2005) versus future model Kolmogorov-Smirnov ensemble totals for the two simulation types (dynamic and static), two emissions scenarios (rcp4.5 and rcp8.5) and three future time periods (2016-2045, 2046-2075, and 2071-2100). The files Stat_gage_performance_conus_new_1951_2009 and Dyn_gage_performance_conus_new_1951_2009 contain the gage performance information from 1951-2009 between observed flow and historical reference simulation for the static and dynamic simulation type respectively. The file Uncertainty_Var_Description.csv contains variable information about data layers included in the PRMS CONUS Futures Portal.
overview.eadetcit: Nikiel, C. A., 2026, Supplementary Data Spanning 1951-2100 to Support the PRMS CONUS Futures Portal, U.S. Geological Survey data release, https://doi.org/10.5066/P13TU2P4.

distinfo.distliab: Unless otherwise stated, all data, metadata and related materials are considered to satisfy the quality standards relative to the purpose for which the data were collected. Although these data and associated metadata have been reviewed for accuracy and completeness and approved for release by the U.S. Geological Survey (USGS), no warranty expressed or implied is made regarding the display or utility of the data for other purposes, nor on all computer systems, nor shall the act of distribution constitute any such warranty.

metainfo.metstdn: FGDC Content Standard for Digital Geospatial Metadata
